column16: S7 FIX (e87ebb3) -> B UPGRADED TO B+. Sub-helpers _road_segment_mesh_spec and _bridge_mesh_spec were previously generating degenerate quads on axis-aligned segments: widths were Y-axis-offset (v0 = sx, sy - hw; v1 = sx, sy + hw) which for a N-S segment (direction along Y) put all four vertices collinear on the Y-axis producing zero-area quads. Now width is offset perpendicular to the segment direction in XY: px = -(ey - sy) / seg_len_xy, py = (ex - sx) / seg_len_xy with safe zero-length fallback. All segments now produce valid non-degenerate quads. Remaining weakness: still flat quads (no contour following).

column17: L265-308: per-vertex 4x4 symmetric quadric matrix via outer product of plane coefficients. Skips degenerate faces (norm < 1e-12). Math is correct: plane = [a,b,c,d] where d = -dot(normal, v0), Q_face = plane plane^T, per-vertex Q = sum of incident Q_face. This is textbook Garland-Heckbert 1997 QEM. Performance: Python for-loop over faces (L283-306) — for a 100k-face mesh this is the slowest part, but np.outer + np.cross are vectorized per-face so the loop overhead is ~0.5s/100k faces. Acceptable for offline LOD bake; would be faster as a fully vectorized face-batch but Python impl is correct.

column17: L311-333: QEM cost at midpoint: Q_combined = Q1+Q2, mid_h = [mx,my,mz,1], cost = mid_h^T Q_combined mid_h. Correct QEM formula — lower cost = cheaper collapse. Uses midpoint as collapse target (simple; full QEM solves for optimal position by inverting Q submatrix, but midpoint is a standard and numerically-safe choice). For a terrain LOD pipeline this IS the silhouette-preserving metric — the right AAA call.
